title: scirpus atrovirens subsp. hattorianus
intro: Scirpus atrovirens subsp. hattorianus, also known as Hattorian's sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Traditionally, Hattorian's sedge is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and roots are used in herbal medicine to create teas and tinctures.
